mnemonics: Self-Reference: 'this' is a pointer to the current object instance, like saying 'this specific object'.
keyPoints: The 'this' pointer is an implicit parameter available in all non-static member functions.
It is a constant pointer, so its value (the memory address it holds) cannot be changed.
It is used to resolve ambiguity between class data members and parameters with the same name, and to return the current object from a member function.
commonMistakes: Attempting to use the 'this' pointer in a static member function, where no object instance exists.
Forgetting to use the 'this' pointer when a member variable is shadowed by a local variable or parameter, leading to unintended behavior.
